text: I draw my sword and approach the sleeping dragon.
I search the abandoned watchtower for anything useful.
I ask the old innkeeper if she has heard any rumors about the haunted forest.
I try to pick the lock on the iron-bound chest.
I offer the wandering merchant my last silver coin for safe passage.
I climb the crumbling tower stairs, listening for footsteps above.
I kneel before the shrine and pray for guidance.
I challenge the bandit captain to single combat.
I follow the trail of blood deeper into the cave.
I light a torch and step into the crypt.
I try to tame the wounded wolf I found in the clearing.
I barter with the blacksmith for a new set of armor.
I negotiate with the ferryman to cross the river before nightfall.
I set a snare trap along the game trail near camp.
I study the runes carved into the temple archway.
I bandage my companion's wound with what herbs I have.
I haggle with the fishmonger over the price of dried fish.
I follow the sound of chanting deeper into the woods.
I inspect the broken wagon wheel for a way to repair it.
I offer the beggar outside the gates a piece of bread.
I climb the old oak to get a better view of the valley.
I trade stories with the caravan guards around the fire.
I search the ruins for a way past the collapsed archway.
I calm the spooked horse before it bolts.
I ask the librarian for any scrolls about the lost kingdom.
I carve a marker into the tree to remember this path.
I share my rations with the hungry traveler.
I test the strength of the rope bridge before crossing.
I listen at the door before entering the abandoned hall.
I polish my shield before the tournament begins.
